page: Amazon Web Services u-6tb1.56xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 224 dedicated physical cores utilizing the x86_64 Intel Xeon 8176M processor architecture running at a base clock speed of 2.1 GHz. The system is equipped with 6144.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing approximately 27.43 GB of RAM per core. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, and it does not include any GPU hardware. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the u-6tb1.56xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, scoring 258,198.57 in stress-ng multi-core and 101,185.21 in PassMark CPU tests. Its memory subsystem achieves top-tier read-only small block cached bandwidth of 18,718.42 GB/sec, alongside large block read and write speeds of 810.48 GB/sec and 378.57 GB/sec respectively. However, single-core performance is relatively modest, with Geekbench single-core scores and PassMark memory scores falling into the lower percentiles. Redis SET operations perform exceptionally well, reaching 261.5 million operations per second.

Given its massive memory footprint and high-bandwidth networking, this instance is designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. It is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, real-time analytics, and complex enterprise resource planning systems. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which is supported by the high baseline network throughput.
description: Amazon Web Services u-6tb1.56xlarge is a memory-optimized instance designed for massive enterprise workloads. Powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features 224 dedicated physical cores of x86_64 Intel Xeon 8176M processors running at 2.1 GHz, paired with 6144.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but provides a robust 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results highlight top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, with stress-ng multi-core scoring 258,198.57 and Redis SET operations reaching 261.5 million operations per second. Conversely, single-core performance and PassMark memory scores are relatively weak. This hardware profile is highly efficient for massive in-memory databases, large-scale data analytics, and enterprise ERP applications requiring extreme memory density.
og_description: Amazon Web Services u-6tb1.56xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 224 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 6 TB of DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, built for massive in-memory databases.

tagline: A high-density memory-optimized platform featuring six terabytes of RAM and two hundred twenty-four dedicated cores for massive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 224 dedicated physical Intel Xeon cores running at 2.1 GHz
6144 GB of high-speed DDR4 memory providing 27.43 GB per core
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Optimized for large-scale in-memory databases and enterprise ERP systems
